channel.title: Notes on LunarWatcher
channel.description: Recent content in Notes on LunarWatcher

item.title: Physics engine design
item.description: (primarly written for an existing project) The main problem with integrating a physics engine architecture on top of the existing planned AI architecture is that they’re separate, but still have overlap. Optimally, the physics engine needs to track all the entities, and the AI controllers track a subset. This difference is unfortunately why things get hard. Each AI controller can get away with a linear container, where the physics engine also need to store its controlled entities in a tree-like data structure, primarily to optimize for click events.
